input.initialPrompt: Analyze this project for incremental build issues. Identify any custom targets that would unnecessarily re-run on every build even when nothing has changed.

data.reasoningText: Let me analyze the project files to understand the build configuration and identify incremental build issues.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

   </PropertyGroup>
 
   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">

       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
     </PropertyGroup>
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
   </Target>
 
   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
     </Exec>
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                      Overwrite="true" />
     <ItemGroup>
       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>
 </Project>

data.content:
Both custom targets have incremental build issues:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — MSBuild has no way to skip it, so it runs every build.
- Writes `DateTime.Now` to `build-timestamp.txt` — the content changes every time, so even with `Inputs`/`Outputs` it would still produce a new file.
- Lower severity since the `.txt` output isn't a `<Compile>` item.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13) — **most damaging**
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — always re-runs.
- **Unconditionally overwrites `git-hash.g.cs`** with `Overwrite="true"`. Even when the git hash is unchanged, the file's timestamp is updated.
- That file is added to `<Compile>`, so **the updated timestamp forces `CoreCompile` to re-run every build**, defeating incremental compilation entirely.

### Summary

| Target | Runs every build? | Breaks `CoreCompile` incrementality? |
|---|---|---|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | ✅ Yes (no Inputs/Outputs) | No (output isn't compiled) |
| `EmbedGitHash` | ✅ Yes (no Inputs/Outputs) | **Yes** — overwrites a `<Compile>` item |

The `EmbedGitHash` target is the critical issue. The fix would be to write to a temp file first, compare it to the existing `git-hash.g.cs`, and only replace it when the content actually changes — plus add `Inputs`/`Outputs` so MSBuild can skip the target entirely when appropriate.
data.reasoningText:
Let me analyze the two custom targets for incremental build issues:

1. **`GenerateTimestamp`** — Runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` with no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so MSBuild always executes it. It writes the current time to `build-timestamp.txt`, which changes on every build, meaning the file always has new content.

2. **`EmbedGitHash`** — Also runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` with no `Inputs`/`Outputs`. It runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes the result to `git-hash.g.cs`. 

The problem is that both targets lack `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, preventing MSBuild from skipping them. `GenerateTimestamp` produces a different value every build since it captures the current time, which breaks incremental compilation. `EmbedGitHash` overwrites its output file unconditionally, updating the timestamp even when the git hash hasn't changed—and since this generated file is part of `<Compile>`, it forces a recompilation.

The solution for `EmbedGitHash` is straightforward: only write the file if the content actually differs from what's already there. For `GenerateTimestamp`, I need to decide whether it truly needs to run every build, and if so, ensure it doesn't invalidate the compilation cache. The `EmbedGitHash` target is actually the more critical problem—it unconditionally rewrites the git hash file even when the hash hasn't changed, which updates the file timestamp and forces the entire `CoreCompile` step to re-run since that file is marked as a compile input. So while `GenerateTimestamp` wastes cycles, `EmbedGitHash` actively breaks incremental compilation for the whole build.
